tagline: One Trivy scan, every ecosystem — a prioritised security report.
summary: A language-agnostic security audit powered by Trivy. One full scan covers dependency CVEs across every ecosystem (npm, pip, Go, Cargo, Composer, Maven, RubyGems, NuGet and more), hard-coded secrets, and IaC misconfigurations, then renders a prioritised Markdown report — fixable vulnerabilities first, with target fix versions. Report-only: it surfaces what to upgrade without ever touching your code.
capabilities: Scans dependency CVEs across every ecosystem Trivy detects, plus OS packages — no per-language setup
Adds hard-coded secret detection and IaC misconfiguration checks (Dockerfile, Terraform, Kubernetes)
Renders a prioritised Markdown report: fixable findings first, with severity and target fix versions
Audits the repo, not local junk — honours .gitignore across submodules and skips git worktrees and test dirs
Report-only and dependency-light — Trivy is the single external tool, parsed with the stdlib (no jq)
Reports vuln-DB freshness, and validates the Trivy version every run (cached daily) — flagging a stale DB or an outdated binary
